signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Vol + Correlation Break
signals.text: USD/JPY at 162.41 — 275bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid despite Nikkei -4.03% rout. JPY safe-haven function is broken (z=-2.00σ vs VIX), with carry flows dominating risk-off sentiment. However, 25d RR at -1.37 and VRP at +3.7% (IV 7.2% vs HV30 3.5%) signal the options market is paying a steep premium for downside protection, likely hedging BoJ intervention risk as price holds above 160.00.
signals.watch_level: Sustained trade above 162.50 or BoJ meeting on 31 Jul

signals.title: EUR/USD — Macro + Positioning + CESI
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1435 — 150bp Fed-ECB carry advantage favors USD while Brent > $90 adds terms-of-trade pressure. COT LF net short -74,637 (long% 33.3%) aligns with a strong negative CESI (-16 net misses), suggesting institutional positioning is correctly fading Eurozone data underperformance. VRP at +2.5% indicates the market is pricing in tail-risk ahead of the ECB meeting.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Retail
signals.text: RBNZ 2.5%. This institutional short-bias is further diverged from retail sentiment (71% short), creating a high-conviction short-squeeze setup if the RBNZ maintains its hawkish stance. Real rate at -0.58% remains accommodative, supporting the case for further tightening.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Oil Correlation + CESI Divergence
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4027 — The pair exhibits a significant correlation break vs WTI Oil (z=-1.90σ), with CAD failing to benefit from the commodity bid. While 150bp carry favors USD, the USD CESI (+48 beats) vs CAD CESI (+2 net) suggests the fundamental divergence is driven by US data outperformance rather than energy prices. BoC pause remains supported by soft inflation headlines.
